title: “royal disease” also known as hemophilia B or Christmas disease
summary: “royal disease” also known as hemophilia B or Christmas disease
detail: You carry a form of hemophilia known as the “royal disease,” transmitted from Queen Victoria (1819–1901) to European royal families. It is currently unknown if this "royal disease" rs398122990(G) SNP is extinct world-wide, but if you're seeing this in your Promethease report, you may be the proof that the variant is not extinct. Please let info@snpedia.com know that you saw this in your report.
whyItMatters: SNPedia has a genotype-specific page for this result, which can add consumer-facing context alongside primary evidence sources.
conditions: “royal disease” also known as hemophilia B or Christmas disease

You carry a form of hemophilia known as the “royal disease,” transmitted from Queen Victoria (1819–1901) to European royal families. It is currently unknown if this "royal disease" rs398122990(G) SNP is extinct world-wide, but if you're seeing this in your Promethease report, you may be the proof that the variant is not extinct. Please let info@snpedia.com know that you saw this in your report.

notes: SNPedia is supplementary and should not be treated as a primary clinical source.

title: Royal Disease Hemophilia B SNP
summary: Royal Disease Hemophilia B SNP
